steps.title: Track the argument
steps.content: The text starts by noting pteropods are thought to be vulnerable to ocean acidification. It then presents Peck and colleagues' findings: the periostracum prevents shell dissolution, and pteropods can rebuild the inner shell wall even when the periostracum is breached. These findings suggest the worry may be overstated.

steps.title: Match the purpose
steps.content:
B captures this exactly: presenting findings that suggest a concern about ocean-acidification effects on pteropod shells may be unwarranted.

A calls for more research (not the focus). C is about behavior changes (not addressed). D narrows to calcium carbonate's role in protecting the periostracum, which inverts the actual relationship.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
